reviews.review_description: The overall look and feel of the game is amazingly fun! Bright and vibrant colors makes the style stand out. I enjoyed the uniqueness for a VR game but I don’t think a casual gamer would like how you control the character. Having the option for an Xbox One controller would be nice.

I want to like this game. I've tried to look past the fact that you can't steer/direct things with the Gear VR/Go controller and find the merit in the developer's lowest common denominator approach, but I just can't. Instead I am once again ultimately perturbed by a failure to properly exploit the platform's capabilities.  And I'm not just talking about utilizing motion controller mechanics but designing the game in such a way that people are free to look around at the environment whenever they like (the current method of requiring the user to pause the game and then subject them to an intrusive message with a huge dark backdrop, along with a delay, in order to enter and exit a free look mode, is almost comically out of alignment with what a VR experience _should_ be).

I like the level design and I like the atmosphere.  But head aiming ultimately just kills it for me.  I can see myself wanting to give it another look if controller steering were to be implemented, but otherwise it's going to lay dormant in my library.  Unfortunately.

Lots to love about this game (so far). The only thing I’d change: the option of using the Go controller as a motion controller to tilt-control the ball’s movement. Though the head nodding to move option works pretty well in the meantime.
Very enjoyable and lots of fun. Reminds me of a futuristic Monkey Ball type game. Well worth buying.

I was really hoping there would be a good marble roll game in VR and this should have been it. The scenery and style is good enough but it really does need the option of controller. I've not played for very long yet but I feel that gaze control will not be accurate enough as the levels progress. Comfortable and relaxed environments but for the price could definitely be better than it is.

reviews.review_title: My neck hurt like hell after 15 minutes
reviews.review_description: This game with controller support (Xbox controller preferably) would be a 5 stars. It is a very well made game that could be fun to play. But a human head was not designed to be used as a joystick and you neck will suffer a lot. It is also frustrating having to hold your head always in a certain orientation without any possibility to relax ever. If you relax your head only 1mm, the ball will fall and you will have to start again. With this much pain in my neck, it is a very frustrating experience. PS : I don't have any problem with my neck, I am in good shape. Another good game turned to disaster by horrible VR control because the DEV want to "innovate" instead of using what actually works.

Like most of these reviews have mentioned, controlling the ball using your head is just tedious and not fun. It's fine during the first levels but the later levels requiring precise movement are a chore.

Another annoying thing to me is all the pop in graphics. Many elements don't appear until you are almost right on them. If this is a style choice, it us a bad one. This reminds me of old consoles like N64 that did not have the power to render all of the environment. Its distracting. 

Would like to give this a better score, and I would if motion controller support was added.

reviews.review_title: Love it!! Needs a Quest 2 port!!
